content.problemStatement:
You are a warrior and you have to fight NNN enemy warriors (numbered 111 through NNN) one by one, in any order you choose. You have to win as many of these fights as possible.

Each warrior has some amount of power, which changes when the warrior fights. For each iii, the iii-th enemy warrior has power PiP_iPi​. When you have power xxx and you fight an enemy warrior with power yyy, the following happens:


if x>yx \gt yx>y, you kill the enemy warrior and your power changes to 2(x−y)2(x-y)2(x−y)
otherwise (if x≤yx \le yx≤y), the enemy warrior kills you
You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, you are given your initial power XXX and you should find the maximum number of warriors you can kill if you are starting with power XXX.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains a single integer XXX describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the maximum number of warriors you can kill.
